mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and pushing S&P 500 returns higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding industrial production signal synchronized real-sector demand recovery. This joint expansion compresses credit spreads and lifts equity risk premiums, generating positive forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative or zero predictive Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising CPI compresses real yields and forces the central bank to maintain restrictive policy. This monetary tightening suppresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of triggered episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands corporate revenue bases. Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently translates into higher earnings per share, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 115.0, supply chain capacity utilization peaks, indicating robust manufacturing output. This supply strength supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or predictive power vanishes when controlling for sector rotation.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth paired with stable unemployment indicates broad-based labor demand without wage-price spiral risks. This healthy labor dynamic supports consumer discretionary spending and lifts equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 2% over any 2-year period, or forward returns show no significant correlation with the joint indicator.

mechanism_narrative: During credit contraction phases, declining residential investment signals a collapse in household balance sheets. This demand shock reduces corporate cash flows and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered episodes, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward test.
